meta.title: Requirements Assistant to standardize intake and reduce rework

problem.context: Requests arrive through multiple channels and requirements are inconsistent across teams.
problem.goal: Create an internal agent that turns free text into a standardized requirements package with traceability.
success_definition: Reduce reopenings caused by unclear requirements by 20%

in_scope: Input via form or pasted text
out_of_scope: Auto-create or modify tickets without approval
assumptions: A point-of-contact exists per area to validate
constraints: No sensitive data to external LLMs (redaction)

requirements.statement: The tool must accept a free-text request and generate a structured requirements package as JSON conforming to schema v1.
requirements.rationale: Standard output enables automation and reduces ambiguity.

evidence.source_excerpt: I want a configurable agent... economical... do not use the LLM for everything.

requirements.statement: The tool must render DOCX and Markdown from the JSON package using local libraries, without using the LLM for rendering.
requirements.rationale: Reduce LLM costs and ensure consistent formatting.

evidence.source_excerpt: It should call native Python document generation libraries.

requirements.statement: The tool must apply redaction before sending content to any external LLM provider, using configurable rules.

evidence.source_excerpt: It will run locally... choose provider... economical.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a valid request, the tool returns JSON that validates against schema v1.
The generated DOCX contains the standard sections populated from JSON without calling the LLM.
Detectable sensitive identifiers are masked before any external LLM call.

security.notes: Run with least privilege, logs, kill switch.

checks: Avoid storing raw text by default

open_questions.question: Which ticketing/backlog system will be used (if any) in later phases?
Which data patterns must always be redacted?

risks.risk: Sensitive data could bypass redaction due to incomplete patterns.
risks.mitigation: Add patterns + tests + block on low confidence.

risks.risk: LLM cost may grow with long prompts and multiple calls.
risks.mitigation: One structured call + cache + strict limits.

decision_log.decision: Pilot runs in read-only mode (no ticket creation).
